metadata.note: All incidents are fictional operational scenarios for public demonstration.

incidents.title: Duplicate submission alert after channel retry
incidents.summary: A synthetic client retried a request without preserving the idempotency key, creating duplicate operational alerts but no duplicate settlement.
lessons: Expose idempotency validation earlier in onboarding.
Add dashboard tile for duplicate-submission trend.

incidents.title: Validation rule pack rejected valid pacs.009 examples
incidents.summary: A synthetic rule-pack update treated an optional settlement field as mandatory for pacs.009 messages.
lessons: Require flow-level sign-off for validation rule changes.

incidents.title: Screening queue age breached synthetic SLA
incidents.summary: Screening adapter retry settings amplified a synthetic downstream slowdown and delayed case creation.

incidents.title: Routing table version missing low-value EUR route
incidents.summary: A synthetic route-table deployment omitted an amount-band route, sending payments to a repair queue.
lessons: Show route table lineage in service detail view.

incidents.title: Delayed synthetic SWIFT acknowledgement reconciliation
incidents.summary: Acknowledgements were received but correlation lag left payments in submitted state longer than expected.
lessons: Add ack-age alert to flow explorer.

incidents.title: Status publisher dead-letter queue growth
incidents.summary: A status reason mapping change sent a subset of synthetic status events to the dead-letter queue.
lessons: Display DLQ ownership in service detail.

incidents.title: Repair cases aged after workbench release
incidents.summary: A synthetic UI release changed repair reason defaults and slowed operator completion.

incidents.title: Knowledge dashboard freshness indicator turned amber
incidents.summary: Synthetic analytics publication lag delayed knowledge-health metrics for the executive dashboard.
lessons: Separate operational freshness from documentation freshness.
Add publisher offset lag to dashboard.
